sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults with acute coronary syndromes, including people treated with percutaneous coronary intervention after myocardial infarction.
sources.clinical_setting: Acute cardiology, discharge, cardiac rehabilitation, and post-myocardial-infarction follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for post-acute-coronary-syndrome and post-myocardial-infarction PCI care; partial for a generic post-PCI workflow because not every PCI follows acute coronary syndrome.

exact_sections.heading: PCI and antithrombotic-risk recommendations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports PCI context and clinician-led bleeding, thromboembolic, cardiovascular, and antithrombotic-risk review without mapping medicine names or doses into generated content.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 and 1.4.10 — secondary prevention and follow-up monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports a clear post-MI management plan, blood-pressure and renal monitoring, and bleeding-risk assessment at follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.34 — discharge information and cardiac rehabilitation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.15 — lifestyle after myocardial infarction
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle discussion after myocardial infarction without autonomously generating diet, activity, smoking, or weight advice.

archive.title: Acute coronary syndromes
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle discussion after myocardial infarction without autonomously generating diet, activity, smoking, or weight advice.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
sources.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult cardiovascular risk and lipid-modification assessment and monitoring; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.5.7 — lipid assessment and baseline tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports full lipid profile, blood pressure, BMI, diabetes status, renal function, liver transaminases, thyroid context, muscle symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.11.12 — treatment response and annual review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented adherence, adverse-effect review, lifestyle and cardiovascular risk discussion, repeat lipid and liver testing, annual medication review, and agreed follow-up without mapping doses.
